task: Provision Flask-capable backend/frontend test environment and rerun targeted route proofs
proof_target: Replay backend session-route shell/proxy tests and frontend play-shell framing tests in an environment with required Flask dependencies.
why: Current container blocks these route-level proofs with ModuleNotFoundError: flask.
expected_evidence: Fresh PASS outputs for the targeted backend and frontend pytest commands, plus any required environment note.

task: Reproduce the full dual-surface player-path contract
proof_target: Demonstrate with tests and/or replay evidence which path is canonical for ordinary players, which path is live-room advisory, how they converge, and how audience classification is exposed.
why: Committed-path vs live-room/player-visible transport discipline remains a bounded proof seam.
expected_evidence: Route-level tests or E2E evidence showing no silent truth divergence across player-visible surfaces.

task: Replay publish -> activation -> shell -> re-entry as one end-to-end chain
proof_target: Fresh evidence that the published artifact identity survives activation, session birth, shell hydration, turn progression, resume, and re-entry.
why: The chain is strongly source-supported but only partly rerun in this pass.
expected_evidence: Integrated route/service/E2E replay with provenance and audience classification visible.

task: Replay preview, evaluation, promotion, rollback, and runtime-health governance flow
proof_target: Show the governed lifecycle working end to end, including findings, revision candidates, preview isolation, evaluation deltas, promotion decision, history, rollback reachability, and runtime-health interpretation.
why: Canonical lifecycle truth is restored, but fresh live replay is still incomplete.
expected_evidence: Admin/operator-facing replay, logs, and validation outputs covering the lifecycle chain.

task: Provision LangGraph/LangChain dependencies and rerun graph-heavy AI-stack proof
proof_target: Replay the skipped breadth-playability and fallback/orchestration-heavy AI-stack tests under a fully provisioned environment.
why: Graph-heavy proof remains environment-limited in this pass.
expected_evidence: PASS or bounded-failure outputs for the graph-heavy test set with explicit dependency context.

task: Demonstrate research/improvement and MCP support without truth-boundary drift
proof_target: Show that research findings, improvement proposals, preview tools, and MCP operator tooling remain visible and useful while still subordinate to publish/runtime authority.
why: The subsystem is now preserved canonically but not equally product-proven.
expected_evidence: Capability-gated flows, traceability, and tests showing no silent canon mutation or second truth boundary.

proof_target: Either prove retirement-safe removal of transitional backend session surfaces or reclassify them explicitly with stable compatibility posture.
why: This remains a named residue in the validation corpus.
expected_evidence: Updated code, tests, and audit record showing resolved ownership and no hidden fallback truth path.

task: Attempt evaluator-independent higher-level closure only after the above replays pass
proof_target: Advance from strong active-slice Level A posture toward a stronger evaluator-independent closure classification.
why: Current evidence does not justify skipping directly to that claim.
expected_evidence: Independent evaluation package or equivalently strong externalized proof corpus tied to the refreshed runtime evidence.
